terms.name: Measurement
terms.definition: Outcome-based metric tracing to strategic objectives

terms.name: KPI
terms.definition: Version-controlled key performance indicator with formula and historical trend

terms.name: Outcome
terms.definition: Mission or community impact result measured with evidence

terms.name: Benchmark
terms.definition: Contextual performance comparison against targets or peers

terms.name: Continuous Improvement
terms.definition: Permanent observe-measure-analyze-implement cycle

terms.name: Root Cause Analysis
terms.definition: Structured investigation identifying causes not symptoms

terms.name: Best Practice
terms.definition: Reusable institutional asset from successful operations

terms.name: Experiment
terms.definition: Hypothesis-driven pilot with recorded results and lessons

terms.name: Innovation
terms.definition: Searchable institutional idea, pilot, or implementation

terms.name: Maturity Model
terms.definition: Independent capability progression across institutional domains

terms.name: Operational Excellence
terms.definition: Quality, consistency, stewardship, and impact evaluation

terms.name: Community Impact
terms.definition: Institutional impact on citizens, communities, and programs

terms.name: Quality Assurance
terms.definition: Searchable evidence from mission, workflow, and governance reviews

terms.name: Improvement Backlog
terms.definition: Prioritized recommendations converted to actionable initiatives

terms.name: Scorecard
terms.definition: Institutional performance view—not individual ranking

terms.name: Institutional Learning
terms.definition: Accumulated knowledge growth and competency development

terms.name: AI Improvement Advisor
terms.definition: Advisory analysis without autonomous approval authority

terms.name: Evolution Timeline
terms.definition: Explainable history of institutional improvements

terms.name: Mission Impact
terms.definition: Outcome-focused measure of mission success

terms.name: Federation Benchmarking
terms.definition: Cross-institution comparison with autonomy protection
